description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined white fuselage and a distinctive multicolored tail fin featuring a gradient of red, orange, yellow, and blue. The aircraft is branded with 'AIR PACIFIC' in bold blue letters along the forward section of the fuselage. It features four underwing turbofan engines, two on each wing, and a low-wing configuration with slightly swept-back wings. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all adorned with the same colorful livery as the tail fin. The cockpit is located at the very front of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible, and the airplane is designed for long-distance travel, typical of wide-body commercial jets.
description: A large white commercial jet with four underwing engines, a colorful tail fin, and 'AIR PACIFIC' branding. The airplane features a streamlined fuselage and swept-back low wings.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, four engines, and the colorful tail fin.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the 'AIR PACIFIC' branding, cockpit windows, and the multicolored tail fin.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, including the vertical and horizontal stabilizers with the colorful livery.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, with clear visibility of the engines, windows, and tail design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with moderately swept-back wings and no visible winglets; standard dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear unit.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of commercial airliners.
